rationale: The query's core transferable method is the exact solution of a binary-state model (Ising) on networks with arbitrary degree distributions via self-consistent equations for aggregate behavior, yielding critical thresholds dependent on moments of the degree distribution. The candidate is a broad review of statistical physics applied to social dynamics, covering many models and phenomena but not specifically applying or replicating the Ising-model machinery (self-consistent equations for critical thresholds tied to degree distribution moments). The candidate's object—social dynamics and collective phenomena—is genuinely different from the query's physical Ising model on random graphs.
